title: Bill.com Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Bill.com** is a SaaS accounts-payable / accounts-receivable platform exposing a REST API at `https://api.bill.com/api/v2`. The newer Connect v3 surface lives at `https://gateway.prod.bill.com/connect/v3`, but every operation supported by this connector is available on v2 (which is what production Bill.com integrations still use).

This connector — `BillcomConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "billcom"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from Bill.com:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Auth | `/Login.json`, `/Logout.json` | Session exchange (4-piece bundle → `sessionId`) |
| Vendors | `/List/Vendor.json`, `/Crud/{Read,Create}/Vendor.json` | List, get, create vendors |
| Customers | `/List/Customer.json`, `/Crud/{Read,Create}/Customer.json` | List, get, create customers |
| Bills (AP) | `/List/Bill.json`, `/Crud/{Read,Create}/Bill.json` | List, get, create bills |
| Invoices (AR) | `/List/Invoice.json`, `/Crud/{Read,Create}/Invoice.json` | List, get, create invoices |
| Payments | `/SendPayment.json`, `/List/SentPay.json`, `/Crud/Read/SentPay.json` | Issue, list, get payments |
| Ledger | `/List/ChartOfAccount.json`, `/List/ActgClass.json`, `/List/Location.json` | Read-only ledger metadata |

The connector normalises vendors + bills + customers + invoices into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), transparently re-logs-in when Bill.com rejects a stale `sessionId` (BDC_1024 / "Invalid Session."), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::BillcomHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Provision a Bill.com developer key

1. Sign in to https://developer.bill.com.
2. Apply for a **Developer Key** (`devKey`). Production keys are issued via a short review; sandbox keys are immediate.
3. Note the `devKey` value — it is **per-integration** (one devKey used across all your tenant's Bill.com orgs).

### 2. Grab your Org ID + login credentials

1. Sign in to https://app.bill.com (use the sandbox at https://app-sandbox.bill.com if testing).
2. Open **Settings → Profile → Organization** and copy the **Organization ID** (17-digit string starting with `008…`).
3. Decide which **user account** the connector will log in as. Common choices: a dedicated service user with the minimum role for AP/AR read+write.

### 3. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Bill.com**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `user_name` (text) | Bill.com login email (the service user above) |
| `password` (secret) | Bill.com login password |
| `org_id` (text) | Organization ID from step 2 |
| `dev_key` (secret) | Developer key from step 1 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.bill.com/api/v2` (use the sandbox URL for testing) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the 4-piece bundle, runs `POST /Login.json`, caches the returned `sessionId`, surfaces a `TokenInfo` for observability, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 4. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which force-clears the cached session and runs a fresh `/Login.json` round-trip. On 2xx envelope status=0 → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On envelope status=1 with an auth code (BDC_1018) → `OFFLINE + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On network error → `OFFLINE + PENDING`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Bill.com REST API v2 uses **session-based authentication** seeded by a 4-piece credential bundle (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"` at the platform level — the bundle is treated as a single secret):

### Credential model

- `user_name` — Bill.com login email (sent as the `userName` form field on `/Login.json`).
- `password` — Bill.com login password (sent as the `password` form field on `/Login.json`).
- `org_id` — Bill.com Organization ID, e.g. `00800000000000000` (sent as the `orgId` form field on `/Login.json`).
- `dev_key` — Bill.com Developer Key (sent as the `devKey` form field on **every** call, including `/Login.json`).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validate the 4-piece bundle. Run `POST /Login.json` to verify the bundle AND cache the first `sessionId`. Surface `TokenInfo(access_token=sessionId, token_type="session")`. |
| `authorize(...)` | No-op — returns the cached `sessionId` wrapped as a `TokenInfo`. |
| `health_check()` | Force a fresh `POST /Login.json` round-trip. |
| `_ensure_session()` | If no cached `sessionId`, login. Otherwise return the cached value. |
| `_call_with_session(fn)` | Run `fn(session_id)`. On `BillcomSessionExpired`, clear the cache, re-login, retry once. |
| token storage | `sessionId` cached in-process (`self._session_id`) + mirrored as `TokenInfo` via `set_token` for observability. |

### Wire contract

Every authenticated call is `POST <path>.json` with `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`:

```
sessionId=<sessionId>&devKey=<devKey>&data=<JSON-encoded-body>
```

`/Login.json` is the only call that does NOT take a `sessionId`; instead it takes `userName`, `password`, `orgId`, `devKey` directly.

### Envelope

Every response is JSON wrapped in:

```json
{
  "response_status": 0,
  "response_message": "Success",
  "response_data": { ... }
}
```

On `response_status=1`, `response_data` is `{error_code, error_message}`. `client/http_client.py::_parse_envelope` is the single owner of envelope decoding.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `user_name` | text | yes | `self.user_name` | `userName` form field on `/Login.json` |
| `password` | secret | yes | `self.password` | `password` form field on `/Login.json` |
| `org_id` | text | yes | `self.org_id` | `orgId` form field on `/Login.json` |
| `dev_key` | secret | yes | `self.dev_key` | `devKey` form field on every call |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.bill.com/api/v2`) | Override for sandbox or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["user_name", "password", "org_id", "dev_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    "auth":    ("OFFLINE",   "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    "session": ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    "network": ("OFFLINE",   "CONNECTED"),
    "rate":    ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_BILLCOM_BASE = "https://api.bill.com/api/v2"
```

`_STATUS_MAP` is keyed by failure category (not HTTP status) because envelope errors arrive on HTTP 200 — the connector classifies failures using the envelope `error_code`/`error_message` fragment first, then maps to the canonical `(ConnectorHealth, AuthStatus)` pair.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `BillcomConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::BillcomHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate the 4-piece bundle. Call `POST /Login.json` to verify credentials AND cache the first `sessionId`. Persist config + token. Returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)` on success; `OFFLINE + MISSING_CREDENTIALS` on bad creds; `OFFLINE + PENDING` on transport failure.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No OAuth exchange — returns the cached `sessionId` wrapped as a `TokenInfo(token_type="session")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Force a fresh `POST /Login.json`. Maps failure categories via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Lists + normalizes vendors, bills, customers, invoices → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

### `async login() -> str`
Force-refresh the Bill.com session and return the new `sessionId`. Used by `health_check()` and the platform when the connector explicitly needs a fresh session.

### `async logout() -> Dict`
Invalidate the current `sessionId` on the Bill.com side via `POST /Logout.json` and clear the in-process cache. No-op when no session is cached.

children.title: Vendors
children.content:
### `async list_vendors(*, start=0, max=99, filters=None) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /List/Vendor.json` — paginate vendors. `filters` accepts either a list of `{field, op, value}` dicts (Bill.com native shape) or a plain dict (coerced to that shape by `normalize_filters`).

### `async get_vendor(vendor_id) -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Read/Vendor.json` — read one vendor.

### `async create_vendor(name, *, email=None, address1=None, city=None, state=None, zip=None, country="US") -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Create/Vendor.json` — create a vendor. Body wraps the fields in `{obj: {entity: "Vendor", ...}}`.

children.title: Bills (Accounts Payable)
children.content:
### `async list_bills(*, start=0, max=99, filters=None) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /List/Bill.json` — paginate bills.

### `async get_bill(bill_id) -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Read/Bill.json` — read one bill.

### `async create_bill(vendor_id, invoice_number, invoice_date, due_date, amount, line_items) -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Create/Bill.json` — create a bill with one or more line items. Body: `{obj: {entity: "Bill", vendorId, invoiceNumber, invoiceDate, dueDate, amount, billLineItems: [...]}}`.

### `async pay_bill(bill_id, payment_date, *, amount=None) -> Dict`
`POST /SendPayment.json` — schedule a payment. Body: `{billId, processDate, amount?}`. If `amount` is omitted, Bill.com pays the full outstanding bill amount.

children.title: Customers + Invoices (Accounts Receivable)
children.content:
### `async list_customers(*, start=0, max=99, filters=None) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /List/Customer.json`.

### `async get_customer(customer_id) -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Read/Customer.json`.

### `async create_customer(name, *, email=None, bill_address1=None) -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Create/Customer.json`.

### `async list_invoices(*, start=0, max=99, filters=None) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /List/Invoice.json`.

### `async get_invoice(invoice_id) -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Read/Invoice.json`.

### `async create_invoice(customer_id, invoice_number, invoice_date, due_date, amount, line_items) -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Create/Invoice.json` — create an invoice with one or more line items.

children.title: Payments + Ledger
children.content:
### `async list_payments(*, start=0, max=99, filters=None) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /List/SentPay.json` — paginate sent payments.

### `async get_payment(payment_id) -> Dict`
`POST /Crud/Read/SentPay.json`.

### `async list_accounts(*, start=0, max=99) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /List/ChartOfAccount.json` — list ledger accounts (cash, AR, AP, expense lines, …).

### `async list_classifications(*, start=0, max=99) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /List/ActgClass.json` — accounting classifications (regions, business units, …).

### `async list_locations(*, start=0, max=99) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /List/Location.json` — entity locations.

*(`list_chart_of_accounts` is preserved as a back-compat alias for `list_accounts`.)*

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
BillcomError                       # base; carries response_code + response_body + status_code
├── BillcomAuthError              # envelope BDC_1011/1018/1019/1020/1021
├── BillcomSessionExpired         # envelope BDC_1024 / 0001 / "Invalid Session" fragments
├── BillcomBadRequestError        # HTTP 400 / envelope validation codes
├── BillcomNotFoundError          # HTTP 404 / envelope BDC_11xx / "not found" fragments
├── BillcomConflictError          # HTTP 409 / envelope duplicate / conflict fragments
├── BillcomRateLimitError         # HTTP 429 / envelope "rate limit" fragments — retry_after_s
└── BillcomServerError            # HTTP 5xx — retry candidate
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
BillcomNetworkError = BillcomServerError      # legacy name from older code
BillcomNotFound     = BillcomNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_post_form` + `helpers/utils.py::with_retry`)

| Layer | Action |
|---|---|
| HTTP 400 / 404 / 409 / 4xx (non-429) | Raise immediately as the most specific subclass |
| HTTP 429 | Honour `Retry-After` (default `0.5s * 2**attempt`); retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3`; surface `BillcomRateLimitError` on exhaustion |
| HTTP 5xx | Exponential backoff (`0.5s * 2**attempt`); retry up to 3; surface `BillcomServerError` on exhaustion |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `BillcomNetworkError` |
| Envelope status=1 + auth code | Raise `BillcomAuthError` (no retry) |
| Envelope status=1 + session-expired code/fragment | Raise `BillcomSessionExpired` → connector layer silently re-logs-in + retries |
| Envelope status=1 + other | Raise `BillcomError` (no retry — operator inspects `response_code`) |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
BillcomAuthError       → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
BillcomSessionExpired  → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
BillcomRateLimitError  → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
BillcomNetworkError    → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)
BillcomError           → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | One of `user_name` / `password` / `org_id` / `dev_key` blank | Re-enter the missing field in ACP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` with `BDC_1018` in the message | Wrong username/password | Verify the service user can log in to https://app.bill.com directly. Password rotated? Update the connector field. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check with `BDC_1011` | `dev_key` invalid or revoked | Confirm with developer.bill.com that the devKey is still active for your org. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` with `BDC_1020` / `BDC_1021` | Service user lacks API access on this org | In Bill.com → Settings → Users → edit the service user → enable API access. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, PENDING)` at install | Network error reaching `api.bill.com` | Check egress allowlist — the host must be reachable from the connector pod. |
| Periodic `billcom.session_expired` warnings | Normal — Bill.com sessions expire (usually 35 min idle). The connector silently re-logs-in and retries; users see no failure. | No action needed unless every call expires (rotate devKey + check clock skew). |
| `billcom.http.5xx_retry` warnings spike | Bill.com upstream incident | Check https://status.bill.com. The client retries with exponential backoff and surfaces `BillcomServerError` after 3 attempts. |
| `billcom.http.rate_limit_retry` warnings spike | Hitting the 60 req/min default cap | Lower `rate_limit_per_min` install field or batch fewer requests. Bill.com may also rate-limit per-user — consider a dedicated API user. |
| `pay_bill` returns BDC_2xxx error | Bill amount / banking validation failed | Inspect `response_code` + `response_message` — Bill.com validates payee bank info, available bank balance, and entity status before scheduling. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'BillcomNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `BillcomNetworkError = BillcomServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Webhook payloads land but no handler runs | Bill.com webhooks are not currently consumed by this connector — they require the v3 Connect surface | Track in the connector roadmap; v3 Connect webhook ingestion is a future enhancement. |
